rag_rules: Fix AGREE syntax errors by correcting invalid characters or input constructs like unmatched brackets.
Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes for port-level properties.
Use visible identifiers and declared helpers to ensure AGREE expressions resolve correctly.
Validate that all AGREE types (int, real, bool) match the expected scalar type of the expression.
Write deterministic dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should be labeled with descriptive strings.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare their types explicitly.
Assignments in AGREE are used for implementation-level bindings in component implementations.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths in component type annexes.
Do not introduce invalid or unresolved identifiers in AGREE expressions without prior declaration.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics related to syntax errors
Component implementation annexes for type-level contracts
